industries.name: Fiber Optic Cables & Infrared Optics
uses: Germanium dioxide (GeO₂) dopes the silica core of single-mode optical fiber, increasing its refractive index slightly above the cladding to create the waveguide — every kilometer of telecom fiber contains ~1g of germanium
Global fiber optic cable deployments for 5G backhaul, datacenter interconnects, and FTTH (fiber to the home) programs collectively consume hundreds of tonnes of germanium annually as dopant

Germanium lenses and windows for thermal infrared (8–12 μm) imaging systems: FLIR thermal cameras, night vision goggles, missile seeker heads, and industrial thermal inspection equipment — germanium is transparent in the thermal IR band invisible to silicon optics
Germanium is the dominant lens material for uncooled microbolometer thermal cameras (VOx detector arrays) in automotive night vision, security cameras, and handheld thermal imagers
Anti-reflection coatings on germanium IR optics using layers of ZnS, ZnSe, or diamond-like carbon to maximize transmission across the 8–14 μm atmospheric window

companies.name: Umicore
companies.use: Major germanium refiner and specialty product producer. Processes germanium from zinc smelter residues and recycled scrap. Produces GeO₂ for fiber optic preform manufacturers and germanium metal for IR optics and semiconductor applications.

companies.name: Vitrus (II-VI, now Coherent)
companies.use: World's largest producer of germanium IR optical components. Manufactures germanium lenses, windows, domes, and prisms for thermal imaging cameras, FLIR systems, and missile seekers supplied to defense and commercial markets.

companies.name: Yunnan Germanium (Chihong)
companies.use: World's largest germanium producer. Recovers germanium from zinc smelting slag at its Chihong zinc-lead smelter in Yunnan province. China controls ~80% of world germanium reserves; export restrictions imposed August 2023 alongside gallium.

industries.name: Solar Cells & Space Power
uses: Germanium wafers as the bottom subcell substrate in triple-junction solar cells (InGaP/GaAs/Ge) for satellite and spacecraft power — these cells achieve 32–35% efficiency versus 22% for silicon, critical where panel area is constrained
Concentrated photovoltaic (CPV) systems: triple-junction cells on germanium substrates under 500–1000× concentrated sunlight achieve record 47%+ efficiency in controlled conditions
Ge-on-Si epitaxial layers in next-generation silicon photonics chips — germanium photodetectors on silicon integrate optical receivers directly on CMOS chips for datacom transceivers

Germanium transistors were the original semiconductor device (Bardeen, Brattain, Shockley, Bell Labs 1947) — now superseded by silicon except in specialized cryogenic and very-high-frequency applications

companies.name: Azur Space Solar Power
companies.use: European manufacturer of triple-junction GaAs/Ge solar cells for satellite power systems. Supplies space-qualified solar cell assemblies to ESA, Airbus Defence & Space, and Thales Alenia Space.

companies.name: Spectrolab (Boeing)
companies.use: World's largest space solar cell manufacturer. Produces XTJ Prime and IMM (inverted metamorphic multi-junction) cells on germanium substrates for NASA, ESA, and commercial satellite programs. Has powered more spacecraft than any other solar cell maker.

industries.name: Electronics, Catalysts & Specialty
uses: Polyethylene terephthalate (PET) polymerization catalyst: germanium dioxide (GeO₂) as alternative to antimony trioxide catalyst for food-contact PET bottles and packaging — produces clearer, antimony-free PET preferred in Japan and premium packaging markets

SiGe (silicon-germanium) heterojunction bipolar transistors (HBTs) in RF chipsets for 4G/5G smartphones — SiGe HBTs outperform silicon at microwave frequencies with lower noise figure for LNA (low-noise amplifier) applications

Germanium-on-insulator (GeOI) substrates for next-generation high-mobility CMOS transistors — germanium's higher electron and hole mobility (2× and 4× silicon) enables faster transistors at lower voltage
Night vision goggle objective lenses: germanium aspheric lenses focus thermal IR in military image intensifier and thermal weapon sight systems

Germanium recovery from recycling: secondary germanium from scrapped IR optical systems, fiber preform manufacturing waste, and PET catalyst residues is an important supply source as primary production is constrained

companies.name: Infineon Technologies
companies.use: Major producer of SiGe BiCMOS chipsets for 4G/5G RF front-end modules in smartphones. Its SiGe technology enables low-noise amplifiers and mixers operating at millimeter wave frequencies for 5G NR.

companies.name: GlobalFoundries
companies.use: Major SiGe foundry offering BiCMOS9 and BiCMOS9MW process nodes for RF, automotive radar, and communications chipsets requiring SiGe HBTs alongside CMOS logic. Supplies SiGe wafers to Qualcomm, Skyworks, and Murata.

companies.name: Tosoh Corporation
companies.use: Produces high-purity germanium dioxide for PET bottle production (catalyst-grade) and for optical fiber preform manufacturing. Major supplier to Japanese PET resin producers and fiber optic preform makers.
